mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real personal consumption expenditures (RSAFS) signals robust consumer demand, which drives corporate revenue and earnings. This trend mechanically lifts S&P 500 valuations over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The rolling 2-year rank IC between RSAFS growth and 20-day forward SPX returns stays below 0.03, or the correlation is not consistently positive.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate climbs above 4.5%, it signals a weakening labor market and rising recession risk. Historically, such thresholds precede negative equity returns as earnings expectations fall and risk premiums rise.
failure_condition: In more than 50% of the trading days where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, the subsequent 20-day SPX return is positive.

mechanism_narrative: When payroll growth is strong (monthly change > 150k) simultaneously with an unemployment rate above 4.0%, it reflects labor market mismatches or rising slack that eventually depresses corporate profits and equity prices.
failure_condition: When the joint condition holds, SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in less than 60% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening regime defined by the federal funds rate exceeding 4%, further increases compress equity valuations through higher discount rates and tighter financial conditions, producing reliably negative forward returns.
failure_condition: In periods when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4%, the fraction of positive 20-day SPX returns is greater than 40%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) signals expanding manufacturing output and broad economic strength. This positive supply-side impulse translates into higher equity prices within a week as corporate earnings expectations adjust.
failure_condition: The rolling 1-year rank IC between INDPRO growth and 5-day forward SPX returns is below 0.03 or not consistently positive.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts exceed 1500 thousand units, the market prices in future oversupply and a potential housing downturn, which depresses consumer wealth and spending, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Less than 50% of the windows triggered by HOUST > 1500 produce negative 60-day SPX returns.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real GDP growth (GDPC1) directly supports corporate earnings and reduces recession fears. This fundamental driver yields positive equity returns over a full quarter as the economic expansion is validated.
failure_condition: The correlation between GDPC1 growth and subsequent 60-day SPX returns is not positive over any 5-year rolling window (Spearman rank < 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: The joint condition of low policy rates (FEDFUNDS below 3%) and elevated consumer spending (RSAFS above $500bn) signals an environment where accommodative monetary policy is actively supporting demand, which reliably lifts equity prices over the next month.
failure_condition: When both conditions hold, the 20-day SPX return is negative more than 40% of the time.
